title: List Functions in Documentation
text: methods_list() methods_inline() generate Rd markup listing methods generic: can use roxygen2 documentation two ways: Compute methods package documented (.e. devtools::document() time), use inline R code roxygen comment: ’ll need add doclisting Suggests. Compute methods documentation rendered (.e. ? time), use Rd code block: ’ll need add doclisting Imports add dummy call methods_list() eliminate R CMD check note:
code: cat(doclisting::methods_list("mean")) #> \itemize{ #>   \item \code{\link[=Dates]{Date}} #>   \item \code{default} #>   \item \code{\link[=difftime]{difftime}} #>   \item \code{\link[=DateTimeClasses]{POSIXct}} #>   \item \code{\link[=DateTimeClasses]{POSIXlt}} #>   \item \code{quosure} (\pkg{rlang}) #> } cat(doclisting::methods_inline("mean")) #> \code{\link[=Dates]{Date}}, \code{default}, \code{\link[=difftime]{difftime}}, #> \code{\link[=DateTimeClasses]{POSIXct}}, #> \code{\link[=DateTimeClasses]{POSIXlt}}, \code{quosure} (\pkg{rlang}) #' @description #' `r doclisting::methods_list("my_generic")` #' @description #' `Rd doclisting::methods_list("my_generic")` ignore_unused_imports <- function() {     doclisting::methods_list }

what: List methods for a generic — methods_list
title: List methods for a generic — methods_list
text: Generate Rd markup list methods generic function. methods_list() generates bulleted list methods_inline() produces comma-separated list, suitable inline use. can use roxygen2 documentation two ways: Compute methods package documented: `r doclisting::methods_list("generic")` need include doclisting Suggests. Compute methods documentation rendered: `Rd doclisting::methods_list("generic")` need include doclisting Imports. Methods package generic linked without package qualifier; methods packages include package name.

title: List methods for a generic — methods_list
code: methods_list(x, package = NULL)  methods_inline(x, package = NULL)
